done_criteria: Story id or target doc path proposed under docs/stories/
Actor, need, expected outcome, scope, and non-goals stated
The change is framed as one delivery slice, not a roadmap bundle
Open questions or missing context captured explicitly when needed
Relevant precedents in docs/stories/, docs/architecture/, and code are named concretely
Affected client, server, shared, db, and docs areas are listed when applicable
Validation commands are chosen from the real repo workflow, including make build, make test, and persistence-related commands when needed
Delivery risks, dependencies, and likely touch points are recorded without inventing architecture
docs/brief.md reviewed and the relevant product guardrails extracted
The proposal is checked against pull workflow, no direct assignment, minimalismo documental, and other applicable constraints
Any conflict with the brief is converted into scope cuts, blockers, or explicit open questions before drafting
Markdown story draft created or updated under docs/stories/
Acceptance criteria are observable and testable
Tasks or subtasks map back to acceptance criteria
Expected code, docs, UI, and persistence touch points are named concretely
The same story file under docs/stories/ is updated with explicit encaje notes for views, routes, components, reusable UI, layout, architecture, and visual structure
Relevant Lustre surfaces, Gleam modules, shared contracts, and persistence concerns are mapped from the repo instead of guessed
The story includes explicit test design for critical paths, additional relevant flows, and meaningful edge cases
If the proposal does not fit the current brief, UX structure, or architecture, the mismatch is narrowed, rebounded, or marked as blocker instead of hidden
Story is small enough for one implementation slice
Blocking ambiguities are called out explicitly or declared absent
Validation plan is reproducible from the repo and names the commands that matter
Brief alignment and solution fit notes remain reflected in the final story
Final verdict is recorded as READY or BLOCKED
